HR9.reason: Climate Action Now Act - directs the President to develop a plan for the US to meet its nationally determined contribution under the Paris Agreement to reduce greenhouse gas emissions

HR2042.reason: Ratepayer Protection Act of 2015 - allows states to opt out of EPA's Clean Power Plan (Obama's key CO2 regulation for power plants), blocking federal CO2 emission standards

HR4775.reason: Ozone Standards Implementation Act of 2016 - delays EPA implementation of updated ozone (smog) standards by extending compliance deadlines, weakening air pollution regulation

HR1030.reason: Secret Science Reform Act - prohibits EPA from using scientific studies unless underlying data is publicly available, designed to hamstring EPA's ability to regulate based on epidemiological and health studies that use confidential patient data

HR1029.reason: EPA Science Advisory Board Reform Act of 2015 - restructures EPA's science advisory process in ways that industry groups supported and environmental groups opposed, adding barriers to EPA regulatory action

HR1023.reason: Repeals section 134 of the Clean Air Act relating to the Greenhouse Gas Reduction Fund, eliminating the EPA's key tool for funding clean energy and reducing greenhouse gas emissions

HR1121.reason: Protecting American Energy Production Act - blocks Biden administration restrictions on oil and gas leasing on federal lands, opposing regulation of fossil fuel production

HR6009.reason: Restoring American Energy Dominance Act - rolls back Biden-era energy regulations and expands fossil fuel production, opposing emission controls

HR4557.reason: Blocking Regulatory Interference from Closing Kilns Act - prevents EPA from enforcing emission standards on brick kilns, blocking regulation of industrial air pollution

HCONRES86.reason: Resolution expressing the sense of Congress that a carbon tax would be detrimental to the US economy - directly opposes carbon pricing as a mechanism to regulate CO2

HCONRES119.reason: Resolution expressing the sense of Congress that a carbon tax would be detrimental to the US economy (115th Congress) - opposes carbon pricing

HCONRES89.reason: Resolution expressing the sense of Congress that a carbon tax would be detrimental to the US economy (114th Congress) - opposes carbon pricing

SJRES14.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule on 'Oil and Natural Gas Sector: Emission Standards for New, Reconstructed, and Modified Sources Review' (Obama-era methane emissions rule) - voting Yea blocks methane emission regulations on oil and gas operations

SJRES11.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule on 'Control of Air Pollution From New Motor Vehicles: Heavy-Duty Engine and Vehicle Standards' - voting Yea blocks EPA emission standards for heavy-duty trucks, opposing CO2 regulation

SJRES31.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule on 'Review of Final Rule Reclassification of Major Sources as Area Sources Under Section 112 of the Clean Air Act' - voting Yea allows major pollution sources to reclassify as area sources to avoid stricter emission controls

SJRES61.reason: CRA disapproval of FHWA rule on 'National Performance Management Measures; Assessing Performance of the National Highway System, Greenhouse Gas Emissions Measure' - voting Yea blocks requirement for states to measure and reduce transportation greenhouse gas emissions

HJRES136.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule on multi-pollutant emissions standards for 2027+ light-duty and medium-duty vehicles - voting Yea blocks tailpipe emission standards that would accelerate EV adoption and reduce CO2

HJRES87.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA waiver allowing California heavy-duty vehicle zero-emission standards - voting Yea blocks California's clean truck rules and prevents other states from adopting them

HJRES88.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA waiver allowing California Advanced Clean Cars II standards - voting Yea blocks California's mandate for zero-emission vehicle sales

HJRES89.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA waiver allowing California low-NOx 'Omnibus' regulation for engines - voting Yea blocks California emission reduction standards for nonroad engines

HJRES35.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule on 'Waste Emissions Charge for Petroleum and Natural Gas Systems' - voting Yea blocks the methane fee on oil and gas operations established by the Inflation Reduction Act

SJRES12.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule on methane waste emissions charge for oil and gas systems - Senate companion to HJRES35, voting Yea blocks the methane fee

HJRES61.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule on 'National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants: Rubber Tire Manufacturing' - voting Yea blocks EPA emission standards for rubber tire factories

SJRES76.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule extending deadlines for oil and natural gas sector climate review - the underlying rule gave more time for compliance with emission standards; disapproving it creates regulatory uncertainty

SJRES86.reason: CRA disapproval of EPA rule on South Dakota regional haze plan - voting Yea blocks EPA's air quality plan requiring emission reductions from power plants and industrial sources to improve visibility

S4072.reason: Prohibits use of funds to implement or enforce certain EPA rules - blocks enforcement of major EPA environmental regulations

HJRES27.reason: CRA disapproval of revised 'Waters of the United States' definition by Army Corps and EPA - voting Yea narrows Clean Water Act jurisdiction, reducing federal environmental regulatory authority

SJRES22.reason: CRA disapproval of 'Waters of the United States' rule by Corps of Engineers and EPA (114th) - voting Yea blocks expanded Clean Water Act jurisdiction over wetlands and waterways

HR1732.reason: Regulatory Integrity Protection Act - blocks EPA's 'Waters of the United States' rule, narrowing Clean Water Act jurisdiction and reducing environmental regulatory authority

HR1435.reason: Preserving Choice in Vehicle Purchases Act - blocks federal and state mandates that could limit the sale of new gas-powered vehicles, opposing transition to zero-emission vehicles

HR4468.reason: Choice in Automobile Retail Sales Act - similar to HR1435, blocks EPA waiver for California's Advanced Clean Cars II standards that would mandate EV sales

HR3632.reason: Power Plant Reliability Act (119th) - weakens or delays EPA emission standards for power plants in the name of grid reliability, opposing CO2 regulation from the power sector

HJRES38.reason: CRA disapproval of the Stream Protection Rule - voting Yea blocks Obama-era rule that required coal mining operations to protect streams from pollution and restore mined land

HJRES36.reason: CRA disapproval of BLM waste prevention rule - voting Yea blocks Obama-era rule requiring oil and gas companies on federal lands to reduce methane venting, flaring, and leaks
